name: ccrypt
title: ccrypt
summary: secure encryption and decryption of files and streams
description:
secure encryption and decryption of files and streams
ccrypt is a utility for encrypting and decrypting files and streams. It was
designed as a replacement for the standard unix crypt utility, which is
notorious for using a very weak encryption algorithm. ccrypt is based on the
Rijndael cipher, which is the U.S. government's chosen candidate for the
Advanced Encryption Standard (AES, see http://www.nist.gov/aes). This cipher is
believed to provide very strong security.

use_cases: When you need Kali Linux, use ccguess because secure encryption and decryption of files and streams.
When the engagement is in the install phase, use ccguess as a cataloged Kali Linux tool with operator ACCEPT/CANCEL gating.
When you need a concrete invocation, run `ccguess [options] file...` as documented upstream for ccguess.
When you need a concrete invocation, run `ccguess -K gardenhose myfile.cpt` as documented upstream for ccguess.
When chaining Kali Linux tools, pair ccguess with sibling catalog entries sharing its attack_surface/phase_hint.
When validating lab setup, run `ccguess --help` (or the README entrypoint) and confirm required hardware/deps from the catalog.
